title: Nonlinear electrical transport phenomena as fingerprints of a topological phase transition in ZrTe5

paper_analysis.summary: This paper studies flux-grown ZrTe5 single crystals using magnetotransport and nonlinear I–V measurements to probe a temperature-driven topological phase transition. The key finding is that both a nonlinear longitudinal response for E // B and a third-order nonlinear Hall response for E ⊥ B emerge below T* ~ 30 K, providing a more sensitive fingerprint of the transition than linear transport; the authors also report scaling relations linking linear and nonlinear conductivities.
measurement_types: nonlinear Hall voltage / nonlinear Hall conductivity

figures.description: Schematic band structure and Brillouin zone of ZrTe5, showing the Dirac band at Γ and the inferred Fermi-energy positions of samples S1–S13.
Temperature dependence of resistivity for representative samples (S11, S12, S13, S1), highlighting a resistivity peak near 40 K in the more insulating samples.
Field dependence of Hall resistivity at 1.7 K for several samples, showing a step-like anomalous Hall effect in S11–S13.
Hall resistivity at 200 K for selected samples, used to estimate carrier density from linear fits.
Scatter plot of absolute carrier density versus residual resistivity, showing that samples with AHE and negative LMR have low carrier density and large residual resistivity.
I-V curves and residuals in the longitudinal configuration (B // I) at B = 9 T, showing nonlinearity only for S11–S13.
I-V curves and residuals at B = 0 T, showing linear behavior.
I-V curves and residuals in the transverse configuration (B ⊥ I) at B = 9 T, showing linear behavior.
I-V curves and residuals in the in-plane Hall configuration at B = 9 T, showing linear behavior.
Schematic of the longitudinal transport geometry (B // I).
Longitudinal magnetoresistance versus magnetic field at different temperatures for S11, showing negative LMR turning positive near 80 K.
Deviation ΔI from linear I-V behavior at different magnetic fields at 1.7 K.
Deviation ΔI from linear I-V behavior at different temperatures at B = 9 T.
Nonlinear longitudinal conductivity ΔσL versus voltage at different magnetic fields.
Nonlinear longitudinal conductivity ΔσL versus voltage at different temperatures.
Magnetic-field dependence of the coefficient b2 extracted from ΔσL, fit to b2(B) = C(2)B2 + C(1)B4.
Temperature dependence of b2 extracted from ΔσL, vanishing at T* ~ 30 K.
Schematic of the transverse transport geometry (B ⊥ I).
Anomalous Hall conductivity versus magnetic field at different temperatures for S11, showing a change in curve shape near T*.
Antisymmetrized nonlinear Hall voltage ΔVxy versus current at different magnetic fields.
Antisymmetrized nonlinear Hall voltage ΔVxy versus current at different temperatures.
Coefficient α from ΔVxy = αI3 as a function of magnetic field.
Coefficient α as a function of temperature, vanishing near T*.
Nonlinear Hall conductance Δσxy versus current at different magnetic fields.
Nonlinear Hall conductance Δσxy versus current at different temperatures.
Log-log scaling of anomalous Hall conductivity σAHE versus longitudinal conductivity σxx for samples S11–S13, showing different exponents in low- and higher-temperature regimes.
Comparison of σAHE versus σxx with known anomalous Hall scaling regimes from other materials.
Scaling relation between b2 and σAHE, including collapse after dividing b2 by B2.8.
Scaling relation between ΔσL and Δσxy at fixed voltage, showing a crossover from linear to quadratic scaling.

paper_analysis.raw_measurement_details: The paper reports raw experimental transport measurements collected on ZrTe5 single crystals: resistivity, Hall resistivity, magnetoresistance, I-V curves, nonlinear Hall voltages, and third-harmonic Hall signals. Measurements were performed in a cryogen-free magnet system from 1.7 to 300 K and B = -9 T to +9 T using a six-probe method, with current source and nanovoltmeter for DC I-V and lock-in amplifiers for harmonic Hall measurements. The paper also reports EDX mapping for compositional verification.

dataset_characterization.data_availability_statement: The paper states that the data supporting the findings are available upon reasonable request from the corresponding author (Heon-Jung Kim; hjkim76@daegu.ac.kr). It also says there are no datasets in published repositories associated with the study. The text additionally contains a contradictory sentence saying data sharing is not applicable as no datasets were generated or analyzed, despite extensive experimental data being described.
dataset_characterization.raw_data_description: Raw data are described only at the level of experimental acquisition: transport traces from six-probe measurements, I-V curves, Hall and magnetoresistance sweeps, and third-harmonic Hall signals. No file formats, column schemas, or repository links are given.
dataset_characterization.processed_data_description: The paper clearly describes processed quantities derived from raw measurements, including residual resistivity ρ0, carrier density n from Hall fits, anomalous Hall conductivity σAHE, nonlinear longitudinal conductivity ΔσL, nonlinear Hall conductivity Δσxy, coefficients b2 and α, antisymmetrized nonlinear Hall voltage ΔVxyA, and residual curves obtained by subtracting linear fits.
dataset_characterization.figure_data_description: Figure-ready processed data are clearly implied for all main figures, but no explicit source-data files, downloadable figure tables, or supplementary data repository are mentioned in the provided text. Supplementary figures are referenced extensively, but their underlying data availability is not described.
dataset_characterization.scripts_description: No analysis scripts, notebooks, or code repositories are mentioned.
dataset_characterization.notes: The paper distinguishes raw transport measurements from processed/fit-derived quantities, but does not provide a formal dataset package. The main data products appear to be experimental curves and derived scaling plots rather than a curated machine-readable dataset. Supplementary figures are used to support heating checks, contact quality, EDX mapping, and additional transport analyses, but the text does not state that their source data are deposited. The data availability section is internally inconsistent: it both offers data on request and says no datasets were generated or analyzed.

classification_prior.raw_data_evidence: The Methods section explicitly describes experimental acquisition of transport data using a cryogen-free magnet system, six-probe method, current source, nanovoltmeter, and lock-in amplifiers, indicating raw measurement traces were collected.
classification_prior.source_data_evidence: No explicit statement about source-data files, figure data tables, or repository-hosted source data is given; only 'available upon reasonable request' is stated.
classification_prior.data_availability_section_relevant: “The data supporting the ﬁndings of this study are available upon reasonable request.”; “No Published Repositories: There are no datasets in published repositories associated with this study.”; “Third harmonic signals in the Hall (transverse) channel were measured using lock-in amplifiers...”
